pattern: h, b, f, ff, l, ll, ss
teachingHint: Your child is learning the sounds 'l' (tongue behind top teeth) and that double letters make one sound: 'll' says lll (same as 'l'), 'ss' says sss (same as 's'). Help your child spot double letters as one sound: be-ll → bell, mi-ss → miss. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

m-i-ss → The 'ss' makes one /s/ sound. Blend: miss
